---
title: "TMP_PW_Inventory_TrialCode"
author: "S.Wilson"
date: "2026-06-09"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(openxlsx)
library(googledrive)

source("parse_porewater_file.R")

drive_path <- "https://drive.google.com/drive/u/2/folders/10z4cDFXpOMRGJQdCWXEKaqXCF8zlKeS1"

```

Need to make this pull in multiple sheets and from google drive 

## Read in the datafiles that you need 

```{r, pull in entered datasheets}

googledrive::drive_ls(path = drive_path, pattern = "Field") -> files

# I think we can use either purrr:map or lapply here but I couldn't figure it out, 
# for now we'll use a for loop

parse_files <- list()

for(i in 1:nrow(files)) {
    
    drive_download(files$id[i], path = "temp.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
    read_excel("temp.xlsx", col_names = FALSE) -> pw_raw
    parse_porewater_file(pw_raw) -> parse_files[[i]]
    unlink("temp.xlsx")

} 

# When you're ready to upload you can use googledrive::drive_upload

```

# Now let's pivot the dataframe so that it can be appended to the inventory
```{r, create sample and vial IDs from entered data to match inventory}

#Grab the columns that you need and pivot them longer
inventory_new <- dat %>%
    pivot_longer(
        cols = c(
            SO4_Cl_H2S,
            Fe,
            DOC,
            NUTR,
            CDOM,
            Isotopes,
            SPE
        ),
        names_to = "Analyte",
        values_to = "Volume_mL"
    )

#We are going to remove rows that were not collected (i.e., zero volume)
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        Volume_mL = na_if(Volume_mL, "-"),
    ) %>%
    mutate(
        Volume_mL = as.numeric(Volume_mL)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Volume_mL))

#We need to recode the SO4 column to match the inventory
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        Analyte = recode(
            Analyte,
            SO4_Cl_H2S = "SO4/Cl/H2S"
        )
    )

#Change the project to match the inventory
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        Project = paste("COMPASS:", Project)
    )

#Change the date into an actual date
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        Collection_Date = as.Date(Collection_Date, format = "%Y%m%d"),
        Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD = format(Collection_Date, "%Y%m%d")
    ) %>%
    mutate(Evacuation_date_YYYMMDD = Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD)

#Add a sample location
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        sample_location = if_else(Analyte == "CDOM", "SEQUIM", "SERC")
    )

#Analyte code to make a sample ID
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        analyte_code = recode(
            Analyte,
            SO4_Cl_H2S = "SO4",
            Fe = "FE",
            NUTR = "NUTR",
            DOC = "DOC",
            CDOM = "CDOM",
            Isotopes = "ISO",
            SPE = "SPE"
        )
    )

#Create a depth code
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        depth_code = paste0(Depth_cm, "cm")
    )

#Now we will create the Sample_ID & Vial ID
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    #Create the Sample_ID
    mutate(
        Sample_ID = paste(
            "TMP",
            case_when(
                Plot == "Control" ~ "C",
                Plot == "Saltwater" ~ "SW",
                Plot == "Freshwater" ~ "FW",
                TRUE ~ NA_character_
            ),
            "PW",
            Grid_Square,
            depth_code,
            analyte_code,
            Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD,
            sep = "_"
        )
    ) %>%
    #Create the Vial_ID
    mutate(
        Vial_ID = paste(
            case_when(
                Plot == "Control" ~ "C",
                Plot == "Saltwater" ~ "SW",
                Plot == "Freshwater" ~ "FW",
                TRUE ~ NA_character_  # or Plot if you want to keep other values
            ),
            Grid_Square,
            Depth_cm,
            analyte_code,
            Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD,
            sep = "_"
        )
    ) %>%
    mutate(Sample_ID = str_replace(
        Sample_ID,
        fixed("_SO4/Cl/H2S_"),
        "_SO4_"
    )) %>%
    mutate(Vial_ID = str_replace(
        Vial_ID,
        fixed("_SO4/Cl/H2S_"),
        "_SO4_"
    ))

#add in the fixed columns and set the order
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        Project = "COMPASS: TEMPEST",
        sample_location = "SERC",
        Volume_mL = as.numeric(Volume_mL)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Volume_mL)) %>%
    select(
        Project,
        Plot,
        Sample_ID,
        Volume_mL,
        Vial_ID,
        sample_location,
        Analyte,
        Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD,
        everything()
    )

#change the Evacuation date to a number
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
    mutate(
        Evacuation_date_YYYMMDD = as.numeric(Evacuation_date_YYYMMDD),
        Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD = as.numeric(Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD),
        Collection_Start_Time_24hrs = as.numeric(Collection_Start_Time_24hrs),
        Collection_End_Time_24hrs = as.numeric(Collection_End_Time_24hrs)
    )
```

# Now prep it to be read into the inventory file 
```{r, process data file to match inventory}

#Read in the master inventory 
master <- read_excel(
  "TEMPEST_PorewaterInventory_May2022_Present.xlsx",
  sheet = "Porewater - Individual",
  skip = 3
)

#clean out any empty sample_ID rows
master <- master %>%
  filter(!is.na(Sample_ID))

# Align the columns
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
  select(any_of(names(master)))

missing_cols <- setdiff(names(master), names(inventory_new))
missing_cols

for (col in missing_cols) {
  inventory_new[[col]] <- NA
}

# Append to the inventory
inventory_new <- inventory_new %>%
  select(names(master))

inventory <- bind_rows(inventory_new, master)

#Set the header rows 
header_rows <- data.frame(
  col1 = c(
    "Project: COMPASS: TEMPEST",
    "Site: SERC",
    "Content: Lysimeter Porewater Inventory",
    "" ))

```

# Replace the sheet in the inventory file 
```{r, add the new inventory file}

# Load existing workbook
wb <- loadWorkbook("TEMPEST_PorewaterInventory_May2022_Present_Trial.xlsx")

# Remove existing sheet if it exists
if ("Inventory" %in% names(wb)) {
  removeWorksheet(wb, "Inventory")
}

# Add replacement sheet
addWorksheet(wb, "Inventory")

# Write header block
writeData(wb, "Inventory", header_rows, startRow = 1, colNames = FALSE)

# Write inventory data below it
writeData(
  wb,
  "Inventory",
  inventory,
  startRow = nrow(header_rows) + 1
)

# Save changes back to the same file

```

# Create sample lists from the new inventory or whole inventory
```{r, create the sample list you need }

#Input the start date, end date, and the analyte you want the list for 
start_date <- as.numeric("20220604")
end_date   <- as.numeric("20260611")
target_analyte <- "Fe" #"SO4/Cl/H2S"


#This will create the sample list and 
sample_list <- inventory %>%
  filter(
    Analyte == target_analyte,
    Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD >= start_date,
    Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD <= end_date
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Plot = factor(
      Plot,
      levels = c("Control", "Saltwater", "Freshwater")
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD, Plot) %>%
  transmute(
    Sample_Number = row_number(),
    Sample_ID, 
    Plot, 
    Collection_Date_YYYYMMDD, 
    Analyte
  )


#Export to print the list 
printable_list <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(printable_list, "Sample List")

#create a header 
writeData(
  printable_list,
  "Sample List",
  paste("TEMPEST Sample List:", target_analyte, start_date, "-", end_date),
  startRow = 1,
  startCol = 1,
  colNames = FALSE
)

writeData(
  printable_list,
  "Sample List",
  sample_list,
  startRow = 2)

#Save the workbook
file_name <- paste0(
  "TEMPEST_",
  target_analyte, "_",
  start_date, "-", end_date,
  ".xlsx")

#export the workbook 
saveWorkbook(printable_list, file_name, overwrite = TRUE)

```
